displayName: Cast Armor Steel (Soviet cast turret, MIL-S-21885)
notes: Cast armor steel per MIL-S-21885. Density 7.85. BHN ~240-260 (softer than rolled RHA). UTS ~700-800 MPa. RHAe ~0.8-0.9 per mm vs KE — inferior to rolled RHA due to casting defects, anisotropy, and lower hardness. Used in T-72 and BMP-series cast turrets. Soviet cast turrets typically 85-90% the ballistic efficiency of rolled plate of same thickness.

source.methodology: RHAe from hardness ratio (250/280=0.89) and published data showing cast armor at 85-90% of rolled RHA ballistic efficiency. Density and tensile per MIL-S-21885.
